title: The water drop effect
wikitext:
[]: The "water drop" effect             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Fri Oct 30, 2009 2:37 pm We know the water drop image is shown in EoE around when we think it's fair to guess Shinji tangs, but it also appears in episode 20 after the mini-complementation bit ("Do you want to become one with me?"x3), at which point Shinji was already shown tanged. I'm guessing the water drop is elsewhere in the series, but I don't recall where. Do you think it has some abstract meaning.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Oct 30, 2009 3:13 pm It's used to mark transition between different worlds/spaces (e.g inner space and real world)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Oct 30, 2009 3:20 pm Pretty much what Xard said.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Fri Oct 30, 2009 8:03 pm but why a water drop?

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Fri Oct 30, 2009 8:33 pm I remember the I Ching trigram thread talked a little about "light reflected off water" and how all that Zen jazz could pertain to instrumentality. I don't remember how often the 'water drop' visual was used, but in the two examples you've listed I can see the correlation. A single drop of water "returning"/"merging" with a larger body parallels what's going on in both EoE's tang sequence and the Ep. 20 stuff. Reflection of light off of the droplet is the same reflection on the ocean yada yada the merging happens yada yada the reflection remains unchanged yada yada the drop and the ocean were one in the same. Also, I thought the water drop that was shown during Ep. 20 came at a point of epiphany for Shinji; even though he was physically dissolved, he still hadn't really figured out wtf was going on yet until that key sequence. I need to watch the series again for verification, though... Might be over-analysis, but I figured it had to be more than just marking a difference between inner/outer worlds. IIRC it was also used in Ep. 23 during Rei's encounter with Armisael (I'd have to look that up for the specific context though).

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Nov 02, 2009 10:09 pm Merridian wrote: Might be over-analysis,

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Nov 05, 2009 7:55 pm The water drop effect always puts me in mind of a bead of sweat quietly falling, then making a loud drip in a quiet place. Along this vein, I think it is used to show that the characters is under stress. The loud echo puts me in mind of something like a sensory deprivation chamber. In a place, alone with your thoughts, which are quite "loud" mentally, a normally quiet drip or disturbance can be quite loud, and can jar you from your thoughts. Perhaps this jarring effect is related to the rescue attempt in the next scene?

I'm guessing the water drop is elsewhere in the series,
